title: Dagu

{{Quote|''[Shaak Ti's]'' actions on Geonosis, Centares, and Dagu were cited by Palpatine in a list of notable accomplishments by the Togruta Jedi Master.|HoloNet News Core Edition report|<ref name="RHNN" />}}
'''Dagu''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="RHNN" /> located in the [[Dagu system/Legends|Dagu system]], a part of the [[Instrop sector/Legends|Instrop sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA Dagu" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|161|Dagu}}</ref> nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after<ref name="Timeline">{{InsiderCite|69|Clone Wars Timeline}}</ref> the conflict's opening [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> it was the site of a revolt<ref name="Timeline" /> and a [[Battle on Dagu|battle]]<ref name="CSWE" /> between the forces of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="RHNN" /> and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="CSWE" /> On the [[Great ReSynchronization calendar|date]] [[21 BBY/Legends|14:3:22]], the [[HoloNet News Core Edition]] reported on a public ceremony held at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on<ref name="RHNN" /> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], in which the Republic's [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] commended the [[Togruta/Legends|Togruta]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] for her valor in, among other actions,<ref name="RHNN" /> protecting the populace of Dagu from the [[Confederacy military/Legends|forces]] of the Confederacy.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The swamp planet Dagu, a remote place where the Confederacy has chosen to build a military base using prisoners of war, seethes with discontent.|A StarWars.com preview article for the then-upcoming novel ''Escape from Dagu''|<ref name="FirstLook">{{SWArchive|url=eu/news/2003/07/news20030718.html|text=First Look: ''Escape from Dagu''}}</ref>}}
[[File:EscapeFromDagu-cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Dagu as depicted in the cover art for the canceled novel ''Escape from Dagu'']]
Dagu was first mentioned in "[[Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:3:22]]," an article written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] as an [[HoloNet News (Insider)|in-universe news report]] that was included in the [[Star Wars Insider 66|sixty-sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine<ref name="RHNN" /> on [[February 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="Insider date">{{SWArchive|url=community/news/2003/02/news20030203.html|text=''Star Wars Insider'' #66}}</ref> Dagu was set to appear in the novel ''[[Escape from Dagu]]'', which would have been written by [[William C. Dietz]] and published in [[March]] [[2004]] as part of the [[Clone Wars multimedia project|''Clone Wars'' multimedia project]]. A [[July 18]], 2003 article on [[StarWars.com]] previewing the novel presented [[Dave Seeley]]'s cover for the book,<ref name="FirstLook" /> which depicted Dagu.<ref name="ArtStation">{{ArtStation|username=daveseeley|url=artwork/G2wa|name=[[Dave Seeley]]|text=Star Wars: Escape From Dagu}}</ref> Ultimately, however, the release of the novel was canceled.<ref name="Insider140">{{InsiderCite|140|Classic Moment|Classic Moment: Shaak Under Attack!}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Dagu [[Star system/Legends|system]], and thereby Dagu itself, in grid square S-16.<ref name="TEA" />

''Escape from Dagu'' would have established Dagu as a remote [[Swamp/Legends|swamp]] planet<ref name="FirstLook" /> that had both open [[Water/Legends|water]] and growths of [[Tree/Legends|trees]] on its surface<ref name="ArtStation" /> and was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Rybet/Legends|Rybet]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The Confederacy of Independent Systems would have been constructing a military base on the planet using the labor of [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] Rybets and Galactic Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] of war, all of whom would be aiming to liberate themselves. Around the time when—unbeknown to the Confederacy's<ref name="FirstLook" /> [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="FirstLook" /><ref name="ArtStation" /> [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Artel Darc]]—Shaak Ti was among the prisoners on Dagu, the Confederacy would have learned of the presence in the prisoners' midst of a [[Unidentified courier|courier]] possessing information critically dangerous to the Republic's existence. Darc would have been dispatched to Dagu to retrieve the [[Courier/Legends|courier]],<ref name="FirstLook" /> and ultimately Ti would have led a prisoner revolt<ref name="Insider140" /> against a force of the Confederacy's [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droids]] at a construction site, in the process engaging in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] against Darc.<ref name="ArtStation" />
